target_flow: Create and manipulate 3D objects in Blender
critical_note: Blender renders its ENTIRE UI in OpenGL. UIA sees only the window (childCount: 0). Use the CONCERTO pattern: keyboard shortcuts for commands/tools/navigation, P6 drag for sculpting/painting/continuous spatial gestures, scroll for zooming, screenshot for reading state. NEVER use drag where a keyboard shortcut exists. NEVER use only one method — blend them per micro-operation.

spatial_gestures.note: Use button:'left' for brush strokes. Provide path as array of {x,y} waypoints with stepDelay:25-40ms for natural strokes.

view_navigation.note: Use /drag with button:'middle' for orbit, /scroll for zoom

examples: F then mouse-move then click to resize brush

reading_state.note: Screenshot is the ONLY way to see what Blender is displaying. Use vision loop: screenshot → decide → act → screenshot → verify.

navigation_plan.note: CRITICAL: mouse must be over 3D viewport for shortcuts to work. Hover at roughly 40% x, 50% y of window.
For EACH micro-operation, pick the right method from concerto_rules. Keyboard for commands, drag for spatial, screenshot for reading.

shortcut_reference.search: F3 — opens command search. Type command name, Enter to execute. This is the universal way to run any Blender command.

add_objects.add_via_search: F3 → type 'Add UV Sphere' / 'Add Cube' / 'Add Cylinder' / 'Add Cone' / 'Add Torus' → Enter

transform.grab_move: G (then optionally X/Y/Z to constrain axis, type number for distance, Enter to confirm)
transform.scale: S (then type number for scale factor, Enter to confirm)
transform.rotate: R (then type number for degrees, Enter to confirm)
transform.constrain_to_axis: After G/S/R, press X, Y, or Z to constrain

modes.object_mode: Tab (toggles between Object and Edit mode)
modes.mode_pie_menu: Ctrl+Tab (pie menu with all modes including Sculpt)
modes.sculpt_mode: Ctrl+Tab → click Sculpt Mode in pie

sculpt.subdivide: Ctrl+1 through Ctrl+6 (adds Multiresolution modifier levels)
sculpt.brush_resize: F → move mouse → LMB to confirm
sculpt.brush_strength: Shift+F → move mouse → LMB to confirm
sculpt.invert_brush: Hold Ctrl while sculpting (carve instead of raise)

sculpt_face_on_sphere.note: CONCERTO EXAMPLE: keyboard for setup, drag for sculpting, screenshot for verification
steps: [keyboard] F3 → 'Add UV Sphere' → Enter — create base sphere
[keyboard] Ctrl+Tab → click Sculpt Mode — enter sculpt mode
[keyboard] Ctrl+4 — add 4 levels of Multiresolution subdivision
[screenshot] /screenshot — verify sphere is subdivided and brush is visible
[keyboard] F → move mouse outward → click — resize brush to ~20% of sphere
[drag] /drag button:left path across nose area — sculpt nose bump
[keyboard] Ctrl+Z — undo if result is wrong, then retry drag
[drag] /drag button:left path for left eye indent — hold Ctrl to carve inward
[drag] /drag button:left path for right eye indent
[drag] /drag button:middle to orbit view — check from different angle

known_issues: UIA returns childCount: 0 — entire UI is OpenGL rendered with zero accessibility
Mouse cursor position determines which panel receives keyboard shortcuts
Shift+A may not work via SendKeys — use F3 search as reliable alternative
Screenshots are the ONLY way to see what Blender is displaying
No text can be copied from the viewport — only screenshots for reading state
Blender 5.x may have different shortcuts than 4.x/3.x
Default cube has only 8 verts — MUST subdivide (Ctrl+3 or higher) before sculpting
Brush may be tiny at default size — always resize with F before sculpting
achievement: First known AI agent to sculpt 3D geometry in Blender. Concerto pattern: keyboard for commands, P6 OS input injection for brush strokes, vision for verification. Zero accessibility tree, full spatial control.
